images.altText: Five men and one woman pose together in an ornate meeting room in the State Department.

children.content: The talks come as both the US and Iran continue to impose their own separate restrictions on the flow of vessels in and out of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: Trump says US-Iran talks could restart within days, Israel-Lebanon summit ends

updates.title: US military says no Iranian-linked ships have made it past Hormuz blockade
Israel and Lebanon agree to conduct direct negotiations
Breaking: Israeli ambassador confirms talks have ended

items.title: IMF releases sobering economic outlook as strait remains disrupted

children.content: The International Monetary Fund paints three economic outlook scenarios that each get progressively worse depending on how long Middle East energy supplies are disrupted.

image.altText: A man walks along a rock wall with ocean and ships in the background.

items.title: Biofuels could bolster fuel security but we export what we need to make them

children.content: Australia controls less than 20 per cent of our liquid fuel supply and relies on imports for the other 80-plus per cent we need to keep the nation moving. But proponents of biofuels argue that it does not need to be this way.

image.altText: Yellow sign states "pump not in use" is attached to the petrol bowser.

items.title: Billionaire's 'slow moving car crash' leads to individuals being investigated by ASIC

children.content: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

image.altText: GFG Alliance executive chair Sanjeev Gupta.

items.title: Trump lashes out at European ally Meloni amid tensions over pope and NATO

children.content: The US president says Giorgia Meloni "does not care" about Iran getting a nuclear weapon, after the Italian PM defended the pope and cancelled a military deal with Israel.

image.altText: Giorgia Meloni and Donald Trump shaking hands.

items.title: Lone backpacker remains on outback station as fuel crisis sees jobs dry up

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

gallery.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.
A sign in an outback town which says "Welcome to Oodnadatta, Australia's hottest driest town."
A large building exterior panted pale pink with two pink canoes outside. The sign reads 'PINK ROADHOUSE OODNADATTA' in black.

avatar.alt: A young woman with brown hair smiles.

items.title: 'Incredibly dangerous' fake weight loss drugs hit UK black market

children.content: Aisleyne Horgan-Wallace lay on her bed, wincing in pain, convinced she was about to die after injecting herself with a cheap, unregulated weight loss drug.

image.altText: Aisleyne Horgan-Wallace sits in a lounge room, on a pink couch with flowers behind her

items.title: Magnitude-4.5 earthquake felt across large parts of NSW

children.content: The NSW Central West has been hit by the largest earthquake ever recorded in the region.

image.altText: map of nsw with blocks showing earthquake

items.title: Judgement day for high-stakes battle of iron ore dynasties

children.content: Gina Rinehart, the wealthiest person in Australia, expects a judgement to be handed down today in an explosive court battle with the family of her father's former business partner over iron ore royalties.

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.
Red dirt at the side of the road with a sign that says 'Hope Downs mine 30km'
Bianca wears a brown top and white skirt as she walks next to John who wears a blue suit

items.title: Collard handed monster ban over 'entirely unacceptable' homophobic slur

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard is suspended after a marathon AFL Tribunal hearing with a deliberation of over 90 minutes.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: Hidden cameras in change rooms cause regional town hall to postpone events

children.content: An investigation into hidden cameras in change rooms has forced the Horsham Town Hall to postpone events in western Victoria.

image.altText: Photo on the corner of the street, looking down two sides of the town hall building with a has a sign: "Horsham Town Hall"

items.title: Carney wins majority government as Canadian by-elections boost Liberals

children.content: The Canadian prime minister said voters had "placed their trust" in his government and its "clear understanding of what this moment demands".

image.altText: A man hugs a woman in a red coat as a crowd takes photos of them.

items.title: Founder of China property giant Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud

children.content: Hui Ka Yan's plea comes after three years in detention, while Evergrande's liquidators are battling in court outside mainland China to freeze the offshore assets of the founder and his ex-spouse over billions of US dollars in dividends and remuneration.

image.altText: A close shot of a man who is China Evergrande Group Chairman Hui Ka Yan.

items.title: Should you turn off your laptop while commuting?

children.content: While modern laptops are good at saving battery life and keeping cool, extended periods in sleep mode could still be putting a strain on them over time.

gallery.altText: A woman standing behind a desk wearing a loose button-up shirt reaches to shut down the lid of a laptop

thumbnail.altText: A woman standing behind a desk wearing a loose button-up shirt reaches to shut down the lid of a laptop

avatar.alt: A headshot of a woman with long light brown hair and blue eyes

items.title: The Australian connection to the 'scariest movie you'll ever hear'

children.content: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

image.altText: A woman in a dimly lit room screams

items.title: Quiz: We're going on a trip — don't forget your toothbrush

children.content: What are the pyramids made out of? And which Caribbean island doesn't appear in the Beach Boys song Kokomo?

image.altText: Hong Kong street at sunset time

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan kick off Australia visit with stop at Melbourne hospital

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have begun their multi-day visit to Australia in Melbourne, meeting with patients at a children's hospital.

articles.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

image.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

gallery.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

thumbnail.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

items.title: The warning signs a health practitioner wants to exploit your superannuation

children.content: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

image.altText: A doctor and nurse in an operating room in a hospital.

items.title: Qantas cuts domestic flights and raises fares as fuel costs blow out

children.content: Qantas will cut domestic flights due to higher fuel costs and the uncertainty of the Middle East war, as it flags as much as $800 million in extra fuel costs.

image.altText: Qantas logo on Sydney Airport terminal glass

items.title: Butters found guilty of abusing umpire as Port claims 'glaring hole in the evidence'

children.content: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters is found guilty of umpire abuse at the AFL Tribunal, despite strongly denying allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?".

image.altText: Zak Butters speaks into microphones

items.title: Man on trial for alleged assault of several women

children.content: Benjamin Bruce, 26, appeared before the WA District Court on charges including sexual penetration without consent and assault against several women.

image.altText: A medium shot with a sign reading: District Court of Western Australia.

items.title: Dramatic dash cam vision of dangerous overtaking prompts safety call

children.content: A 21-year-old woman was charged with reckless driving after dash cam video allegedly caught her overtaking a triple road train in an "unsafe" manner.

image.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

gallery.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

thumbnail.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

items.title: Coalition's migration proposal faces practical questions

children.content: The Coalition's new migration policy aims to increase screening of visa applicants and "boot out" people who do not abide by Australia's values, but questions remain about how the plan would work in practice.

image.altText: Angus Taylor at a press conference in Parliament House.

items.title: Fuel excise cut 'too focused on politics', analysts say as concerns grow

children.content: Some energy analysts are questioning the federal government's temporary tax cuts on fuel, warning that lower prices may work against Australia's long-term needs and goals.

image.altText: Five red petrol pumps in a bowser; one with a red handle, then a blue, a green, a dark blue and a black handle.

items.title: Palestinian groups take Australia to court over Israel arms exports

children.content: A group of Palestinian human rights organisations launch legal action against the defence minister seeking transparency over Australia's arms exports to Israel.

image.altText: A grey fighter jet close up in teh sky.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein to face retrial on New York rape charge

children.content: Former Hollywood magnate Harvey Weinstein is again going on trial on a rape charge in New York City. He will remain imprisoned for other offences regardless of the verdict.

image.altText: A bald man sits in court wearing a suit

items.title: Blow to cartography in Australia as The Map Shop prepares to close

children.content: One of Australia's few remaining cartography shops is about to close in Adelaide, prompting questions about where people can buy the critical documents, particularly those travelling in remote areas where mobile coverage is limited.

Man leaning over a large map with maps behind him
Man in map shop points to metal drawers

thumbnail.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps
Man leaning over a large map with maps behind him
Man in map shop points to metal drawers

items.title: WA children to get needle-free flu vaccine

children.content: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

image.altText: A young boy receives a nasal spray flu vaccine

items.title: Teacher concedes he was 'angry' before 'accidentally' striking student

children.content: A former Darwin teacher who allegedly struck a female student in the face has taken the stand in the Northern Territory Supreme Court, conceding he was "angry" after she swore at him.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

items.title: 'Fierce and defiant' disability advocate remembered after drowning

children.content: Ellen Fraser-Barbour is being remembered for her tireless advocacy and love of nature after her death at the age of 35.

image.altText: A disability advocate with her friend.

items.title: Inquiry told school to lose students if $1.5-billion waste incinerator built

children.content: A school in the middle of a $22-million master plan for its growing enrolment numbers is worried the buildings will not be needed if a waste to energy plant is approved in the town.

image.altText: A man in a suit standing on a shopping street

items.title: Killers chauffeured interstate after Gavin Preston underworld hit, prosecutors allege

children.content: Prosecutors allege two men were chauffeured back to NSW after conducting a contract killing of the Melbourne underworld figure in 2023.

image.altText: A pale photo of an unsmiling, bald man between two blurred figures.

items.title: Australia's largest cattle feedlot on the market as grain-fed beef surges

children.content: Mort & Co, which fattens hundreds of thousands of cattle on grain before being slaughtered for beef, puts the majority of its assets on the market.

image.altText: Aerial photo of a large feed lot.

items.title: Rescuers free young whale after 24-hour stranding ordeal

children.content: A whale stranded on a sandbar in Wallis Lake on the NSW mid north coast has been freed by marine animal rescue teams.

gallery.altText: Four people in Sea World wetsuits and another person in life jacket and helmet standing in water near a stranded whale

thumbnail.altText: Four people in Sea World wetsuits and another person in life jacket and helmet standing in water near a stranded whale
